channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mississippi bill would pay bounty hunters to catch undocumented immigrants
item.description: A Mississippi District Attorney proposed new legislation on Wednesday to pay bounty hunters a reward for helping to deport undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KREX 5

item.title: Marilyn Manson won't face domestic violence and sexual assault charges, DA says
item.description: "We recognize and applaud the courage and resilience of the women who came forward to make reports and share their experiences, and we thank them for their cooperation and patience with the investigation," the district attorney's office said in a news release explaining the decision not to pursue ch
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KREX 5

item.title: Soprano Lise Davidsen pregnant with twins, cancels performances from mid-March through December
item.description: Soprano Lise Davidsen is pregnant with twins and is canceling her performances from mid-March through the end of the year. “I’m very excited about their arrival, and my new life with them,” she said in a statement Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KRON4

item.title: Rocker Marilyn Manson won't be charged after long investigation of sexual assault allegations
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors said Friday that they will not file charges against Marilyn Manson after a years-long investigation of allegations of sexual assault and domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Flame thrower and 'flusher' truck: Southern cities get creative to clear snow, ice
item.description: Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mississippi state lawmaker introduces 'Contraception Begins at Erection Act' with fines for masturbation
item.description: A Democratic Mississippi state senator introduced legislation this week that makes it unlawful for men to masturbate "without the intent to fertilize an embryo," with the lawmaker criticizing anti-abortion measures that only "focus on the woman’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: ICE agents raid NJ seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: "I asked them [the agents] what documentation they were looking for, and they said it was a license or a passport.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump targets California water policy as he prepares to tour LA fire damage
item.description: As President Donald Trump prepares to tour wildfire damage in California, he's zeroing in on one of his frequent targets for criticism: State water policy. Since the fires broke out Jan.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Active-duty troops begin arriving at US-Mexico border in Texas and California to combat migrant crisis
item.description: Active-duty military personnel began arriving along the southern border Thursday evening after President Donald Trump declared a national emergency to address the immigration crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Asylum-seekers pushed to new extremes in Mexico after Trump's border crackdown begins
item.description: Migrants in Mexico who were hoping to come to the U.S. are adjusting to a new and uncertain reality after President Donald Trump began cracking down on border security.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky And Edgar H. Clemente, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about the ruling blocking Trump's order on birthright citizenship
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally has faced the first of what will be many legal tests.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson And Mike Catalini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Snowmen replace sandcastles on Alabama beach after historic winter storm
item.description: Many people who don't normally get to experience snow found unique ways to enjoy the rare sight this week as a historic winter storm moved across the South.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally, calling it “blatantly unconstitutional” during the first hearing in a multi-state effort challenging the order.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 7 police officers wounded in Texas shooting
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Seven San Antonio police officers were shot Wednesday night while responding to a “suicide in progress” call and a heavy presence of police were near an apartment complex trying to detain the suspect, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit claims Indiana Catholic school turned blind eye to child porn scheme; failed to protect students
item.description: And the lawsuit also claims that Bishop Luers officials not only knew all about the scheme - supposedly perpetrated by at least three boys and involving dozens of girls - but failed to report it to authorities while also trying to stonewall giving any updates of a supposed investigation to the girls
item.dc:creator: Jeff Wiehe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jury convicts serial killer truck driver in 2007 murder
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — It took a Marion Superior Court jury less than 90 minutes to convict a serial killer truck driver of the murder of an Indianapolis woman more than 17 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look for favorable jurors on charges he fired gun at former friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nearly a hundred jurors packed into a Los Angeles courtroom again on Wednesday with the possibility that they’ll serve on the trial of A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Communities walk tightrope as Trump ramps up immigration enforcement, threatens tariffs
item.description: The mayor El Paso and his chief of police on Wednesday said they will honor calls from federal law enforcement partners in need of assistance but will not volunteer municipal officers for immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly half of remote workers say they'd quit before returning to office full-time
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Nearly half of workers who currently work remotely at least some of the time would likely leave their jobs if ordered to return to the office full-time. At least, that's the key finding in a new survey from the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Starbucks' policy change flushes out a debate over public restroom access
item.description: Starbucks’ decision to restrict its restrooms to paying customers has flushed out a wider problem: a patchwork of restroom policies that has left Americans confused and divided over who gets to use the loo and when. Rules about restroom access in restaurants vary by state, city and county.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump demands apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: President Donald Trump early Wednesday morning slammed the reverend at a National Cathedral prayer service for the inauguration who called on him to have mercy on transgender children and immigrant families.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man arrested at Utah nursing home decades after Hawaii murder
item.description: A man was arrested at a Utah nursing home on suspicion of second-degree murder in connection to a decades-old case from Hawaii.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Napuunoa, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump wants to make showerheads and toilets flow greatly again, but so may utility bills
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump once expressed concern with how low showerhead flow affected his “perfect” hair. Now back in the White House, he's again taking aim at some high-efficiency household items — and that may mean higher water and electric bills in your home.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card, Pirates says massive trade deal stands
item.description: The card, which contains the "Debut Patch" worn by Paul Skenes, quickly became a highly-coveted item. After months of search, an 11-year-old has reportedly come up with it.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Illinois among 22 states suing to stop Trump's order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: CHICAGO — Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul on Tuesday joined three other states in a lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump's executive order that seeks to end birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, KTLA 5

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: This house can protect itself during a wildfire
item.description: You've seen buildings and homes with fire sprinklers inside but what about on the roof? "It does save homes," said Harry Statter of Frontline Wildfire Defense, a company that makes an app and a system that can help defend your home from wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5
